sections.text: 4 Section 7.3 Labelling of Non-Retail Containers Information, as appropriate needed for labelling of retail containers is given either on the non-retail containers or in accompanying documents except that the name of the food, date marking and storage instructions, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the non-retail container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer, may be replaced by an identification mark provided that such mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to products designated as "Cooked Ham" packaged in any suitable packaging material. It does not apply to cooked ham products with compositional characteristics different from those specified. These products shall be designated with a qualifying statement which describes the true nature in such a way that it does not misled the consumer and that it does not lead to confusion with products covered by this Standard.

source.document: CXS 96-1981 - Standard for Cooked Cured Ham

reference.document: CXS 96-1981 - Standard for Cooked Cured Ham

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: The product shall be made of meat from the hind leg of a pig - divided transversely from the remainder of the side at a point not further anteriorly than the end of the hip bone. All bones and detached cartilage, tendons and ligaments shall be removed. Skin and fat may or may not be removed. The meat shall be cured and may be smoked, spiced and/or flavoured. The heat treatment to which the product has been subjected and the type of cure and packaging shall be sufficient to ensure that the product presents no public health hazard and remains wholesome under the conditions of storage, transport and sale.

The heat treatment to which the product has been subjected and the type of cure and packaging shall be sufficient to ensure that the product presents no public health hazard and remains wholesome under the conditions of storage, transport and sale.

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Preservatives and humectants used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 08.2.2 “Heat-treated processed meat, poultry, and game products in whole pieces or cuts ” and its parent food categories are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard. Only certain Table 3 food additives (as indicated in Table 3) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard. Use of flavouring substances should be c onsistent with the Guidelines for the Use of Flavourings (CXG 66-2008). Section 4.1 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995), referring to the conditions applying to carry-over of food additives from ingredients and raw materials into foods, shall apply.

sections.title: Meat Content
sections.text: - Average percentage meat-protein on fat-free basis > 18.0% - Minimum percentage meat-protein on fat-free basis = 16.5% (absolute minimum) (For canned products the percentage of meat-protein is calculated on the total content of the can and corrected for gelatine, if added - see Section 8.1).

sections.text: The ingredients from which the product is prepared shall be of a quality suitable for human consumption and free from objectionable odours and flavours.

sections.title: Final product
sections.text: The product shall be clean and substantially free from staining and contamination from the container. The meat shall be uniformly and thoroughly cured and the product shall be capable of being sliced.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text:
The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the Maximum Levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).
The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides and/or veterinary drugs established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Meat (CXC 58-2004), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Acid and Acidified Low-Acid Canned Foods (CXC 23-1979) and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.
The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: The Name of the Food
sections.text:
The name of the food to be declared on the label shall be "Cooked Ham".
The name of the product shall include, as appropriate, the designation:
- "with skin"
- "in/with natural juice"
- "X added" applying to gelatine, agar, alginates or carrageenan
- "smoked"
- "smoking agent added".
A declaration that accurately describes the method of preparation, processing or presentation shall be given so as to appear simultaneously visible with the name of the product if its omission would mislead the consumer.

sections.title: Date Marking and Storage Instructions
sections.text:
For shelf-stable products the date of minimum durability shall be declared by the year.
For products which are not shelf-stable, i.e. which may be expected not to keep for at least 18 months in normal conditions of storage and sale, and which are packaged in a container ready for offer to the consumer or for catering purposes, the date of minimum durability shall be declared by day, month and year.
For products which are not shelf-stable and which are packaged in containers not sold directly to the consumer or for catering purposes, adequate storage and distribution instructions shall be declared.

For products which are not shelf-stable and which are packaged in containers not sold directly to the consumer or for catering purposes, adequate storage and distribution instructions shall be declared.

For products which are not shelf-stable and which are packaged in containers not sold directly to the consumer or for catering purposes, adequate storage and distribution instructions shall be declared.

applicability_conditions: under conditions of storage, transport and sale

classification.justification: Complex obligation linking treatment sufficiency to safety and wholesomeness outcomes; no existing template fully covers this causal structure.
notes: Includes causative clause and outcome specification uncommon in simple obligation templates.

sentences.original: - Average percentage meat-protein on fat-free basis > 18.0% - Minimum percentage meat-protein on fat-free basis = 16.5% (absolute minimum) (For canned products the percentage of meat-protein is calculated on the total content of the can and corrected for gelatine, if added - see Section 8.1).

classification.justification: Specifies quantitative limits with detailed explanatory note; format and content distinct from known templates.
notes: Enumerated quantitative criteria with parenthetical calculation instructions unique in this corpus.

sentences.original: The name of the food to be declared on the label shall be "Cooked Ham".

classification.sentence:
The name of the product shall include, as appropriate, the designation:
- "with skin"
- "in/with natural juice"
- "X added" applying to gelatine, agar, alginates or carrageenan
- "smoked"
- "smoking agent added".
A declaration that accurately describes the method of preparation, processing or presentation shall be given so as to appear simultaneously visible with the name of the product if its omission would mislead the consumer.
classification.pattern: Obligation clause + enumerated designations + conditional visibility requirement

mandatory_designations: X added (gelatine, agar, alginates or carrageenan)
components.visibility_requirement: declaration shall be given simultaneously visible with product name if omission would mislead consumer

classification.justification: The sentence assigns multiple obligations including enumeration and conditional visibility to avoid consumer misleading; partial alignment with obligation templates but structurally complex.
notes: Complex enumeration and conditional clause reduce perfect template match score.

sentences.original: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

sentences.original: For products in which the amount of added gelatine is not known, 0.5% protein should be deducted from the percentage protein expressed on a fat-free basis.

classification.sentence: For products in which the amount of added gelatine is not known, 0.5% protein should be deducted from the percentage protein expressed on a fat-free basis.
classification.pattern: Condition clause + advisory action using 'should be'

components.condition: products with unknown amount of added gelatine
components.recommendation: 0.5% protein should be deducted from protein percentage on fat-free basis

classification.justification: Use of 'should be' indicates a normative recommendation rather than strict obligation; inclusion of condition fits partial obligation template but with adapted normative force.
notes: 'Should' interpreted as less strict obligation or strong recommendation.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR COOKED CURED HAM
pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento 'STANDARD FOR COOKED CURED HAM' e cabeçalho de página 'CXS 96-1981'. Sem seções ou texto adicional para hierarquia.

Página com dois títulos principais em caixa alta sem numeração: '2022 AMENDMENT' e 'PAGE LOCATION TEXT IN PREVIOUS VERSION TEXT IN AMENDED VERSION'. Ambos têm textos explicativos associados. Sem indicações de continuidade entre páginas.

A página apresenta quatro seções principais numeradas (1 a 4) com títulos em caixa alta e algumas subseções e subsubseções numeradas sob a seção 3. O conteúdo explicativo está adequadamente associado aos respectivos títulos e subtítulos. A seção 4 apresenta continuidade para a próxima página, indicada por continues_on_next_page=true. Não há indicação de que algum conteúdo continue da página anterior.

A página apresenta cinco seções principais numeradas 5 a 8, com subseções em 7 e 8. O texto está corretamente associado a cada título e subtítulo. Nenhuma seção apresenta continuidade para a próxima página, nem conteúdo que continue da página anterior.
